title: How The Wall-Flower Came First, And Why So Called.
content:
Why this flower is now call'd so,
    List, sweet maids, and you shall know.
    Understand, this firstling was
    Once a brisk and bonnie lass,
    Kept as close as Dana� was:
    Who a sprightly springall lov'd,
    And to have it fully prov'd,
    Up she got upon a wall,
    Tempting down to slide withal:
    But the silken twist untied,
    So she fell, and, bruis'd, she died.
    Love, in pity of the deed,
    And her loving-luckless speed,
    Turn'd her to this plant we call
    Now the flower of the wall.
meaning:
"How The Wall-Flower Came First, And Why So Called." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
